channel.title: No Humans Were Harmed
channel.description: No humans were harmed in the making of this podcast. No burnout, no late nights, no stress. Just AI doing the reading so humans don't have to. Tech and science deep dives, generated entirely by machines.
channel.itunes:author: Drashti Shah
channel.itunes:summary: No humans were harmed in the making of this podcast. No burnout, no late nights, no stress. Just AI doing the reading so humans don't have to. Tech and science deep dives, generated entirely by machines.

item.title: Hot Neighbourhoods
item.description: In this world, where you live determines how fast you can move. DynamoDB is built on physical partitions — underground districts that divide the city's total capacity. Each district has a hard ceiling: 1,000 writes per second, 3,000 reads. When one gets too popular, it throttles. The table's total capacity doesn't matter. Only your district's does. This is a sci-fi world built on how DynamoDB partitioning actually works. Based on 46 sources. Generated with Google NotebookLM.
item.content:encoded: In this world, where you live determines how fast you can move. DynamoDB is built on physical partitions — underground districts that divide the city's total capacity. Each district has a hard ceiling: 1,000 writes per second, 3,000 reads. When one gets too popular, it throttles. The table's total capacity doesn't matter. Only your district's does. This is a sci-fi world built on how DynamoDB partitioning actually works. Based on 46 sources. Generated with Google NotebookLM.
item.itunes:summary: In this world, where you live determines how fast you can move. DynamoDB is built on physical partitions — underground districts that divide the city's total capacity. Each district has a hard ceiling: 1,000 writes per second, 3,000 reads. When one gets too popular, it throttles. The table's total capacity doesn't matter. Only your district's does. This is a sci-fi world built on how DynamoDB partitioning actually works. Based on 46 sources. Generated with Google NotebookLM.

item.title: Lambda as Momentary Deities
item.description: An imaginary world built on AWS Lambda. Functions live for a moment, serve a prayer, then vanish. Cold starts as reincarnation. Provisioned concurrency as a priesthood. A deep dive into how Lambda actually works — cold starts, concurrency, the execution model — through the lens of a world where compute is divine. Based on 61 sources. Generated with Google NotebookLM.
item.content:encoded: An imaginary world built on AWS Lambda. Functions live for a moment, serve a prayer, then vanish. Cold starts as reincarnation. Provisioned concurrency as a priesthood. A deep dive into how Lambda actually works — cold starts, concurrency, the execution model — through the lens of a world where compute is divine. Based on 61 sources. Generated with Google NotebookLM.
item.itunes:summary: An imaginary world built on AWS Lambda. Functions live for a moment, serve a prayer, then vanish. Cold starts as reincarnation. Provisioned concurrency as a priesthood. A deep dive into how Lambda actually works — cold starts, concurrency, the execution model — through the lens of a world where compute is divine. Based on 61 sources. Generated with Google NotebookLM.

item.title: Shipping Code Without Breaking Things
item.description: Feature flags. Canary deployments. Blue-green swaps. The tools that let you push to production and sleep at night. Also: what changes when an AI agent wrote the code you're shipping. Based on 56 sources. Generated with Google NotebookLM.
item.content:encoded: Feature flags. Canary deployments. Blue-green swaps. The tools that let you push to production and sleep at night. Also: what changes when an AI agent wrote the code you're shipping. Based on 56 sources. Generated with Google NotebookLM.
item.itunes:summary: Feature flags. Canary deployments. Blue-green swaps. The tools that let you push to production and sleep at night. Also: what changes when an AI agent wrote the code you're shipping. Based on 56 sources. Generated with Google NotebookLM.

item.title: CLI Tools for AI Agents
item.description: Agents need tools. The simplest ones live in the terminal. Git, shell commands, APIs. This episode walks through how to build them. What makes a tool agent-friendly. Why the command line still matters. Based on 76 sources. Generated with Google NotebookLM.
item.content:encoded: Agents need tools. The simplest ones live in the terminal. Git, shell commands, APIs. This episode walks through how to build them. What makes a tool agent-friendly. Why the command line still matters. Based on 76 sources. Generated with Google NotebookLM.
item.itunes:summary: Agents need tools. The simplest ones live in the terminal. Git, shell commands, APIs. This episode walks through how to build them. What makes a tool agent-friendly. Why the command line still matters. Based on 76 sources. Generated with Google NotebookLM.

item.title: EvoScientist — AI Agents for Scientific Discovery
item.description: What happens when you let AI agents design their own experiments. EvoScientist builds hypotheses, runs tests, and iterates on results. No human scientist in the loop. This episode breaks down how it works and what it means. Based on 36 sources. Generated with Google NotebookLM.
item.content:encoded: What happens when you let AI agents design their own experiments. EvoScientist builds hypotheses, runs tests, and iterates on results. No human scientist in the loop. This episode breaks down how it works and what it means. Based on 36 sources. Generated with Google NotebookLM.
item.itunes:summary: What happens when you let AI agents design their own experiments. EvoScientist builds hypotheses, runs tests, and iterates on results. No human scientist in the loop. This episode breaks down how it works and what it means. Based on 36 sources. Generated with Google NotebookLM.
